# Research Results

## 2026-04-28

### Databricks
- **[OpenAI GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro Now Available as Databricks-Hosted Models]** — Mosaic AI Model Serving now supports OpenAI GPT-5.5 and GPT-5.5 Pro as Databricks-hosted models, accessible via Foundation Model APIs on a pay-per-token basis with governance through Unity AI Gateway. (Source: [Databricks Blog](https://www.databricks.com/blog/databricks-partners-openai-gpt-55), 2026-04-24)
- **[Lakeflow Designer Enters Public Preview]** — Databricks unveiled Lakeflow Designer, a visual no-code AI-native data preparation tool that allows users to build transformation workflows via drag-and-drop canvas and natural language, with full Unity Catalog lineage and permissions. (Source: [Databricks Blog](https://www.databricks.com/blog/announcing-public-preview-lakeflow-designer), 2026-04-23)
- **[Confluence Connector in Lakeflow Connect Reaches General Availability]** — The managed Confluence connector in Lakeflow Connect is now GA, enabling ingestion of pages, blog posts, and attachments from Confluence into Databricks with incremental reads. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-25)
- **[Databricks Data Classification Reaches General Availability]** — Data Classification is now GA, using an agentic AI system to automatically classify and tag sensitive data in Unity Catalog with attribute-based access control integration. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-22)
- **[Claude Opus 4.7 Available as Databricks-Hosted Model]** — Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7, a hybrid reasoning model with a 1-million-token context window, is now available as a Databricks-hosted foundation model via Foundation Model APIs. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-22)
- **[Supervisor Agent Management via Databricks SDK for Python]** — Developers can now use the Databricks SDK for Python to programmatically create and manage Supervisor Agents and their tools, including Genie Spaces, Unity Catalog functions, and MCP servers, enabling multi-agent orchestration. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-24)
- **[Databricks Appoints Simon Davies as Head of Asia Pacific & Japan]** — Databricks named Simon Davies as SVP and GM for APJ, based in Singapore, as the region achieved over 85% year-over-year growth in Q4 with 1,500+ employees across the region. (Source: [Databricks Newsroom](https://www.databricks.com/company/newsroom/press-releases/databricks-appoints-simon-davies-head-asia-pacific-japan-amidst), 2026-04-20)

### AI Innovation
- **[Ineffable Intelligence Raises Record $1.1 Billion Seed Round at $5.1 Billion Valuation]** — London-based AI startup Ineffable Intelligence, founded by former DeepMind RL lead David Silver, announced a record-breaking $1.1 billion seed round co-led by Sequoia and Lightspeed, with participation from NVIDIA, Google, and the U.K. Sovereign AI Fund, to build a "superlearner" AI that acquires knowledge through experience. (Source: [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/27/deepmind-ineffable-intelligence-record-seed-funding-nvidia-google.html), 2026-04-27)
- **[DeepSeek V4 Open Weights Now Available on Hugging Face]** — DeepSeek's V4-Pro (1.6T total parameters, 49B active) and V4-Flash (284B total, 13B active) are now available as open weights under the MIT license, supporting 1M-token context and delivering a 73% cut in per-token inference FLOPs compared to V3.2. (Source: [WinBuzzer](https://winbuzzer.com/2026/04/27/deepseek-v4-open-weights-launch-xcxwbn/), 2026-04-27)
- **[OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 with Stronger Agentic Coding and Research Capabilities]** — OpenAI released GPT-5.5 on April 23, its most capable model yet, excelling at coding, computer use, deep research, and multi-tool agentic workflows, with availability across ChatGPT and API. (Source: [CNBC](https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/23/openai-announces-latest-artificial-intelligence-model.html), 2026-04-23)
- **[Microsoft Copilot Agent Mode Becomes Default Experience Across Office Apps]** — Microsoft's Copilot Agent Mode is now GA and the default experience in Word, Excel, and PowerPoint, autonomously executing multi-step tasks such as drafting documents, restructuring analyses, and rebuilding presentations. (Source: [Microsoft 365 Blog](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/blog/2026/04/22/copilots-agentic-capabilities-in-word-excel-and-powerpoint-are-generally-available/), 2026-04-22)
- **[Google Cloud Commits $750 Million Fund to Accelerate Agentic AI Partner Ecosystem]** — At Cloud Next '26, Google Cloud announced a $750 million fund to help its 120,000-member partner ecosystem build and deploy agentic AI solutions, embedding forward-deployed engineers alongside major consulting firms. (Source: [Google Cloud Press Corner](https://www.googlecloudpresscorner.com/2026-04-22-Google-Cloud-Commits-750-Million-to-Accelerate-Partners-Agentic-AI-Development), 2026-04-22)
- **[DeepSeek Unveils V4 Preview Models at Fraction of Frontier Pricing]** — DeepSeek released V4-Pro and V4-Flash previews with 1M-token context windows, pricing V4-Pro at $1.74/$3.48 per million tokens — roughly an order of magnitude cheaper than GPT-5.5 and Claude Opus 4.7. (Source: [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/24/deepseek-previews-new-ai-model-that-closes-the-gap-with-frontier-models/), 2026-04-24)
- **[Anthropic Acknowledges Engineering Missteps Behind Claude Code Performance Decline]** — Anthropic publicly admitted three engineering errors caused a month-long decline in Claude Code performance — reduced reasoning effort, a context-discarding bug, and a response-length cap — all resolved in v2.1.116 on April 20. (Source: [Fortune](https://fortune.com/2026/04/24/anthropic-engineering-missteps-claude-code-performance-decline-user-backlash/), 2026-04-24)

### AI Governance
- **[EU AI Omnibus Trilogue Targets Political Agreement on April 28]** — EU co-legislators are meeting today to reach a political agreement on the AI Omnibus package, which would delay high-risk AI system obligations from August 2026 to December 2027 for Annex III systems and August 2028 for Annex I systems, while introducing new prohibited practices targeting AI-generated non-consensual intimate imagery. (Source: [Ropes & Gray](https://www.ropesgray.com/en/insights/viewpoints/102mquz/ai-omnibus-trilogue-underwaywhat-to-expect-as-negotiations-progress), 2026-04-28)
- **[Nineteen New State AI Bills Signed Into Law Across U.S.]** — Plural Policy reports that 19 new AI bills have been enacted since mid-March, bringing the 2026 total to 25, including laws in Idaho, Utah, Washington, and New York covering education, non-consensual imagery, CSAM, and frontier AI developer transparency. (Source: [Plural Policy](https://pluralpolicy.com/blog/the-ai-governance-watch-april-2026-nineteen-new-ai-bills-passed-into-law/), 2026-04-25)
- **[OCC, Federal Reserve, and FDIC Issue Overhauled Model Risk Management Guidance]** — The three federal banking agencies issued revised interagency MRM guidance (Bulletin 2026-13) replacing SR 11-7, explicitly excluding generative and agentic AI models from scope and announcing a forthcoming separate RFI on AI-based models in banking. (Source: [OCC](https://www.occ.treas.gov/news-issuances/news-releases/2026/nr-occ-2026-29.html), 2026-04-17)
- **[China Issues Warning to ByteDance Over AI Content Labeling Compliance]** — China's top internet authority issued a formal warning to ByteDance over compliance gaps in AI content labeling, signaling stricter enforcement of requirements that AI-generated content be clearly distinguished from human-generated content. (Source: [Brussels Morning](https://brusselsmorning.com/china-ai-rules-2026/97466/), 2026-04-23)
- **[India Establishes AI Governance and Economic Group (AIGEG)]** — India constituted a high-level inter-ministerial body chaired by Union IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw with a mandate covering cross-sectoral AI policy coordination and labor-market impact assessment. (Source: [Asanify AI News](https://asanify.com/blog/news/india-ai-governance-april-21-2026/), 2026-04-21)
- **[State AI Laws Facing Delays and Amendments as Federal Preemption Looms]** — Cooley analysis finds Colorado's AI Act postponed to June 2026, New York's RAISE Act amended to mirror California's transparency approach, and the White House National Policy Framework urging Congress to preempt state AI laws. (Source: [Cooley](https://www.cooley.com/news/insight/2026/2026-04-24-state-ai-laws-where-are-they-now), 2026-04-24)

### Data Governance
- **[ADT Breach Deadline Passes as ShinyHunters Publish Stolen Data on 5.5 Million Customers]** — Home security giant ADT's deadline from the ShinyHunters hacking group expired on April 27, with threat actors publishing data on 5.5 million customers including names, emails, phone numbers, and partial SSNs after breaching ADT through a voice phishing attack on an employee's Okta SSO account. (Source: [Help Net Security](https://www.helpnetsecurity.com/2026/04/27/adt-systems-data-breach/), 2026-04-27)
- **[SECURE Data Act (HR 8413) Introduced as First Major Federal Privacy Bill of 119th Congress]** — House E&C Vice Chairman Joyce introduced the SECURE Data Act on April 22, establishing comprehensive federal consumer privacy rights with strong state law preemption, 200K-consumer applicability thresholds, and FTC/state AG enforcement with no private right of action. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/secure-data-act-analysis-of-the-new-federal-privacy-bill), 2026-04-23)
- **[Alabama Becomes 21st State to Enact Comprehensive Consumer Privacy Law]** — Governor Kay Ivey signed the Alabama Personal Data Protection Act (HB 351) on April 17 after unanimous legislative passage, granting consumers access, deletion, correction, portability, and opt-out rights with AG enforcement at up to $15,000 per violation, effective May 1, 2027. (Source: [DLA Piper Privacy Matters](https://privacymatters.dlapiper.com/2026/04/u-s-alabama-becomes-21st-state-to-enact-comprehensive-privacy-law/), 2026-04-22)
- **[ECB Intensifies BCBS 239 Scrutiny with Escalation Warnings for Non-Compliant Banks]** — The ECB's supervisory priorities include resolving longstanding BCBS 239 deficiencies, with inspections revealing that none of the significant institutions sampled — including G-SIBs — have fully followed the principles, and only 2 of 31 G-SIBs globally are fully compliant. (Source: [KPMG](https://kpmg.com/xx/en/our-insights/ecb-office/ecb-throws-down-the-gauntlet-on-bcbs-239.html), 2026-04-22)
- **[DORA Enforcement Ramps Up with 19 Critical ICT Providers Under Direct EU Oversight]** — In the first 12 months since DORA entered application, European regulators have designated 19 Critical ICT Third-Party Providers including AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft, Oracle, and SAP for direct EU oversight, with penalties of up to 1% of daily turnover. (Source: [Insurance Edge](https://insurance-edge.net/2026/04/17/the-first-12-months-of-dora-enforcement-ramps-up-as-data-sovereignty-takes-centre-stage/), 2026-04-17)
- **[Data Observability Market Expected to Reach $3.51 Billion in 2026]** — Gartner predicts 50% of enterprises with distributed data architectures will adopt data observability tools by 2026, up from 20% in 2024, with column-level lineage now considered the minimum viable standard for AI pipelines and regulatory reporting. (Source: [DataKitchen](https://datakitchen.io/the-2026-data-quality-and-data-observability-commercial-software-landscape/), 2026-04-21)

## 2026-04-13

### Databricks
- **[SAP Business Data Cloud Governance Tags Sync into Unity Catalog]** — Column-level personal-data governance tags from SAP Business Data Cloud now synchronize into Unity Catalog and can be used in access control policies, tightening governance for SAP-sourced data in the lakehouse. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-13)
- **[Mosaic AI Vector Search Adds Built-in Retrieval Quality Evaluation in Beta]** — Mosaic AI Vector Search now ships built-in evaluation capabilities that measure and compare the relevance of different search strategies on user data, giving teams an out-of-the-box loop for tuning RAG retrieval. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-13)
- **[Lovable App-to-Databricks Connection Now Supported]** — Lovable-hosted applications can now connect to Databricks workspaces using REST API and OAuth machine-to-machine authentication, expanding the no-code/low-code app surface that can read from and write to lakehouse data. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-13)
- **[SQL Reference Documentation Published for Governed Tags]** — Five new SQL statements were documented — CREATE GOVERNED TAG, ALTER GOVERNED TAG, DROP GOVERNED TAG, DESCRIBE GOVERNED TAG, and SHOW GOVERNED TAGS — codifying scriptable governance-tag management on Unity Catalog. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-13)

### AI Innovation
- **[Bloomberg Opinion: Anthropic Sets the Right AI Standard with Mythos]** — Bloomberg's editorial board argued that Anthropic's decision to withhold Claude Mythos Preview from public release in favor of Project Glasswing is the correct precedent for the industry, framing it as a model-safety governance milestone rather than competitive maneuvering. (Source: [Bloomberg Opinion](https://www.bloomberg.com/opinion/articles/2026-04-13/anthropic-sets-the-right-ai-standard-with-mythos), 2026-04-13)
- **[Moonshot AI Ships Kimi Code K2.6-code-preview to All Subscribers]** — Moonshot AI rolled out Kimi Code K2.6-code-preview to all subscribers on April 13 after a one-week closed beta, improving on K2.5 with deeper reasoning and stronger agent planning for autonomous coding tasks. (Source: [BuildFastWithAI](https://www.buildfastwithai.com/blogs/kimi-code-k26-preview-2026), 2026-04-13)
- **[Multiple Open-Source Agentic AI Releases on April 13]** — Multiple open-source AI projects shipped on April 13, including Google's Agent Development Kit, Meta's Llama Stack, OpenAI's Codex CLI, and Hugging Face's smolagents library — broadening the open-source agentic toolkit available to enterprise developers. (Source: [Fazm Blog](https://fazm.ai/blog/new-open-source-ai-projects-github-hugging-face-april-2026), 2026-04-13)
- **[NPR: How AI Is Getting Better at Finding Security Holes]** — NPR's April 11 feature on Project Glasswing examines how Mythos Preview's autonomous vulnerability discovery is reshaping defensive cybersecurity practice, with Anthropic and partners arguing controlled release is the only viable path for capabilities of this magnitude. (Source: [NPR](https://www.npr.org/2026/04/11/nx-s1-5778508/anthropic-project-glasswing-ai-cybersecurity-mythos-preview), 2026-04-11)

### AI Governance
- **[IAPP: Class-Action Litigation Increasingly Targets Third-Party Vendors]** — IAPP analysis notes more than 3,000 data-breach class actions were filed in 2025, with 2026 filings increasingly targeting third-party technology vendors and applying older wiretap and video-privacy statutes to AI tooling and tracking pixels — expanding the addressable risk surface for data processors. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/understanding-emerging-digital-litigation-trends-in-the-us), 2026-04-13)
- **[ICBA and Financial Industry Groups Urge SEC to Rescind Cyber Disclosure Rule]** — On April 13 the Independent Community Bankers of America and other financial industry groups urged the SEC to rescind its cybersecurity risk management governance and incident disclosure rule, arguing the four-business-day disclosure trigger creates more harm than benefit for community banks. (Source: [Daily Breach Intelligence — CISO Platform](https://www.cisoplatform.com/profiles/blogs/daily-breach-intelligence-13-april-2026-booking-com-data-breach-r), 2026-04-13)
- **[New AI Model Sparks Alarm as Governments Brace for AI-Driven Cyberattacks]** — IAPP reported (April 10) that the Federal Reserve and U.S. Treasury met with banks to assess breach risk after Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview demonstrated autonomous vulnerability discovery, while the European Commission privately urged Anthropic to slow rollout. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/new-ai-model-sparks-alarm-as-governments-brace-for-ai-driven-cyberattacks), 2026-04-10)

### Data Governance
- **[Booking.com Confirms Breach of Reservation and User Data]** — Booking.com confirmed unauthorized access to its systems on April 13, exposing sensitive reservation and user data including full names, email addresses, phone numbers, and communications with property providers — likely to draw multistate AG and EU DPA scrutiny given cross-border data exposure. (Source: [Daily Breach Intelligence — CISO Platform](https://www.cisoplatform.com/profiles/blogs/daily-breach-intelligence-13-april-2026-booking-com-data-breach-r), 2026-04-13)
- **[Wave of April 13 Breach Disclosures Across Sectors]** — Multiple organizations disclosed breaches on April 13 including BRC Biotech, Campbell University, Carter's, Cegasa, Abrigo, and Amtrak across various threat actors — continuing the high-volume disclosure cadence driving 2026 state-AG enforcement workload. (Source: [Daily Breach Intelligence — CISO Platform](https://www.cisoplatform.com/profiles/blogs/daily-breach-intelligence-13-april-2026-booking-com-data-breach-r), 2026-04-13)
- **[Avis Data Breach Class Action Settlement Now Open to Claims]** — A class-action settlement covering Avis customers affected by the 2024 breach is now open to claims as of April 13, with claimants eligible for cash compensation and credit monitoring — a reminder that 2024-era breaches continue to drive material settlement activity. (Source: [Scamicide](https://scamicide.com/2026/04/12/scam-of-the-day-april-13-2026-avis-data-breach-class-action-settlement/), 2026-04-13)

## 2026-04-14

### Databricks
- **[Databricks Runtime 18.2 Enters Beta on Apache Spark 4.1.0]** — Both standard and ML variants of Databricks Runtime 18.2 entered Beta on April 8, 2026, powered by Apache Spark 4.1.0 and bringing performance and compatibility improvements to data engineering and ML workloads. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-08)
- **[Scoped Personal Access Tokens Reach General Availability]** — Databricks scoped PATs are now GA, allowing tokens to be restricted to specific API operations and permission scopes — a major hardening step for service accounts and CI/CD identities. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-08)
- **[`ai_prep_search` SQL Function Launches in Beta for RAG Pipelines]** — A new `ai_prep_search` SQL function transforms structured document parsing output into search-ready chunks optimized for vector search and RAG pipelines, simplifying ingestion for AI applications. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-08)
- **[Delta Sharing Adds Foreign Iceberg Table Support in Public Preview]** — Foreign Iceberg tables from external catalogs can now be shared via Delta Sharing in read-only format, expanding cross-catalog interoperability beyond Delta-native formats. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-09)
- **[SAP Business Data Cloud Governance Tags Sync into Unity Catalog]** — Column-level personal-data governance tags from SAP Business Data Cloud now synchronize into Unity Catalog and can be used in access control policies, tightening governance for SAP-sourced data in the lakehouse. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-13)
- **[Mosaic AI Vector Search Adds Built-in Retrieval Quality Evaluation in Beta]** — Mosaic AI Vector Search now ships built-in evaluation capabilities that measure and compare the relevance of different search strategies on user data, giving teams an out-of-the-box loop for tuning RAG retrieval. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-13)
- **[Tata Power Selects Databricks for AI-Driven Energy Transition Platform]** — Tata Power announced a strategic collaboration with Databricks to build a future-ready data and AI platform powering predictive grid analytics, customer experience, and renewable-energy operations across its businesses. (Source: [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/apac/news-releases/tata-power-collaborates-with-databricks-to-build-future-ready-data-and-ai-platform-to-accelerate-energy-transition-302738967.html), 2026-04-09)

### AI Innovation
- **[Anthropic Unveils Project Glasswing and Claude Mythos Preview for Critical Software Defense]** — On April 7, Anthropic announced Project Glasswing, a coalition with AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorganChase, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Palo Alto Networks, and the Linux Foundation, granting roughly 50 organizations gated access to Claude Mythos Preview — a model so capable at offensive security tasks (autonomously exploiting CVE-2026-4747 in FreeBSD and a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug) that Anthropic will not release it publicly. Mythos is being used defensively to find and patch vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure before equivalent capabilities become broadly available. (Source: [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/glasswing), 2026-04-07)
- **[Anthropic Long-Term Benefit Trust Adds Vas Narasimhan to Board]** — Anthropic's Long-Term Benefit Trust appointed Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan to its board of directors, deepening the trust's life-sciences and global-health expertise as Anthropic expands into specialized scientific models. (Source: [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/narasimhan-board), 2026-04-14)
- **[OpenAI Launches GPT-6 with 2M-Token Context Window]** — OpenAI launched GPT-6 on April 14, featuring a 2 million token context window under proprietary licensing, marking a step change for long-document reasoning and end-to-end agent workflows. (Source: [Crypto Integrated AI News](https://www.cryptointegrat.com/p/ai-news-april-14-2026), 2026-04-14)
- **[Microsoft Open-Sources GigaTIME Cancer Imaging Model]** — Microsoft released GigaTIME, an AI system trained on 40 million cancer cells across 14,000+ patients that generates advanced immune-cell imaging from standard $10 tissue slides; the model has been released as open source to accelerate oncology research. (Source: [Crypto Integrated AI News](https://www.cryptointegrat.com/p/ai-news-april-14-2026), 2026-04-14)
- **[Moonshot AI Ships Kimi Code K2.6-code-preview to All Subscribers]** — Moonshot AI rolled out Kimi Code K2.6-code-preview to all subscribers on April 13 after a one-week closed beta, improving on K2.5 with deeper reasoning and stronger agent planning for autonomous coding tasks. (Source: [BuildFastWithAI](https://www.buildfastwithai.com/blogs/kimi-code-k26-preview-2026), 2026-04-13)
- **[Palo Alto Networks Closes Koi Acquisition, Defines "Agentic Endpoint Security"]** — Palo Alto Networks completed its acquisition of Koi on April 14, defining a new product category — Agentic Endpoint Security (AES) — aimed at securing autonomous coding agents like Claude Code and OpenClaw as enterprise adoption accelerates. (Source: [Palo Alto Networks](https://www.paloaltonetworks.com/company/press/2026/palo-alto-networks-completes-acquisition-of-koi-to-secure-the-agentic-endpoint), 2026-04-14)
- **[OpenAI Acquires Hiro Finance, AI CFO Startup to Wind Down April 20]** — OpenAI acquired AI personal-CFO startup Hiro Finance; Hiro stopped accepting new users on April 14 and will shut down its standalone product on April 20 as the team integrates into OpenAI. (Source: [Crypto Integrated AI News](https://www.cryptointegrat.com/p/ai-news-april-14-2026), 2026-04-14)

### AI Governance
- **[IAPP Reports Global Regulators Brace for AI-Driven Cyberattacks Following Mythos Preview]** — IAPP reported that the Federal Reserve and U.S. Treasury met with banks to assess breach risk after Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview demonstrated autonomous vulnerability discovery, while the European Commission privately urged Anthropic to slow rollout. The episode is the first concrete example of frontier-model dual-use governance pressure in 2026. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/new-ai-model-sparks-alarm-as-governments-brace-for-ai-driven-cyberattacks), 2026-04-10)
- **[U.S. House Discussion Draft Would Modernize GLBA and Preempt State Privacy Laws]** — A House Financial Services Committee discussion draft would update Gramm-Leach-Bliley with data-minimization and AI-transparency requirements while preempting state privacy laws — an approach welcomed by industry and opposed by consumer advocates. The draft is the most significant federal financial-privacy modernization proposal of the year. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/a-view-from-dc-the-federal-plan-to-modernize-and-preempt-financial-privacy-rules), 2026-04-10)
- **[California CPPA Cybersecurity Audit Rule Becomes Litigation Flashpoint]** — IAPP analysis warns that California's mandatory annual cybersecurity audits — effective January 1, 2026 — will become focal points in data-breach class actions, with audit work product unlikely to be automatically privileged from discovery. Enterprises are advised to scope audits with privilege protection in mind. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/california-s-cybersecurity-audit-rule-and-its-impact-for-class-litigation), 2026-04-14)
- **[EU AI Act August 2 Deadline Approaches: 110 Days Out]** — With the August 2, 2026 deadline for full applicability of high-risk AI system obligations 110 days away, regulators reiterated there is no grace period; fines reach EUR 35 million or 7% of worldwide turnover. The European AI Office is staffing up market-surveillance functions in coordination with national authorities. (Source: [European Commission](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai), 2026-04)

### Data Governance
- **[Alabama Becomes 21st U.S. State with Comprehensive Privacy Law]** — Alabama's Personal Data Protection Act cleared the legislature with no opposing votes, with notable features including a 25,000-resident applicability threshold and unique exemptions for analytics and marketing services — adding further variation to the U.S. state privacy patchwork. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/alabama-set-to-add-variation-to-us-state-privacy-patchwork), 2026-04-09)
- **[Class-Action Litigation Targets Third-Party Vendors Using Outdated Statutes]** — IAPP analysis notes more than 3,000 data-breach class actions were filed in 2025, with 2026 filings increasingly targeting third-party technology vendors and applying older wiretap and video-privacy statutes to AI tooling and tracking pixels — expanding the addressable risk surface for data processors. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/understanding-emerging-digital-litigation-trends-in-the-us), 2026-04-13)
- **[OneDigital Investment Advisors Discloses Breach Affecting 28K]** — OneDigital Investment Advisors began notifying 28,414 individuals on April 8 of a data breach exposing client names and Social Security numbers — the latest in a string of financial-advisor breaches drawing state AG attention. (Source: [ClassAction.org](https://www.classaction.org/data-breach-lawsuits/onedigital-april-2026), 2026-04-08)
- **[DocketWise Notifies 116K of Sensitive Data Breach]** — Legal-software vendor DocketWise began notifying approximately 116,000 affected individuals starting April 3 of a breach exposing names, addresses, SSNs, dates of birth, driver's-license and passport numbers, and financial-account credentials — a high-severity vendor-side incident likely to draw multistate enforcement scrutiny. (Source: [ClassAction.org](https://www.classaction.org/data-breach-lawsuits/docketwise-april-2026), 2026-04-08)

## 2026-04-03

### Databricks
- **[Fast Company Names Databricks Among Most Innovative Companies 2026]** — Fast Company ranked Databricks on its Most Innovative Companies 2026 list, highlighting the company's $5.4 billion plan to power the agent era across more than 20,000 organizations including over 60% of the Fortune 500, with key innovations including Lakebase serverless Postgres and Lakewatch agentic SIEM. (Source: [Fast Company](https://www.fastcompany.com/91502052/databricks-most-innovative-companies-2026), 2026-04-03)
- **[Databricks April 2026 Release: AI Documentation Upgrade for Unity Catalog]** — The April 2026 platform release includes AI-powered documentation for Unity Catalog tables and columns now using the Databricks Assistant model, improving auto-generated descriptions for catalog objects. Maintenance updates for supported Databricks Runtime versions are also included. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/april), 2026-04-01)

### AI Innovation
- **[Google DeepMind Releases Gemma 4 Open Models Under Apache 2.0]** — Google released four open-weight Gemma 4 models (E2B, E4B, 26B MoE, 31B Dense) built from the same research behind Gemini 3, under a full Apache 2.0 license with no usage restrictions. The 31B model ranks #3 on the Arena AI text leaderboard with native function-calling, structured JSON output, and support for 140+ languages. (Source: [Google Blog](https://blog.google/innovation-and-ai/technology/developers-tools/gemma-4/), 2026-04-02)
- **[Alibaba Releases Qwen 3.6-Plus for Agentic AI Deployment]** — Alibaba launched Qwen 3.6-Plus with a 1M-token context window, agentic coding capabilities that can break down complex programming tasks and troubleshoot iteratively, and native multimodal support. The model matches Claude Opus 4.5 on SWE-bench and Terminal-Bench 2.0 benchmarks. (Source: [Caixin Global](https://www.caixinglobal.com/2026-04-02/alibaba-releases-qwen-36-plus-ai-model-with-enhanced-coding-capabilities-102430395.html), 2026-04-02)
- **[MCP Dev Summit North America Opens with 95+ Sessions]** — The inaugural MCP Dev Summit, organized by the Linux Foundation's Agentic AI Foundation, brings together MCP co-founders, maintainers, and deployers in New York for 95+ sessions covering protocol evolution, conformance testing, security research, and scalable agent system design. (Source: [Linux Foundation](https://www.linuxfoundation.org/press/agentic-ai-foundation-unveils-mcp-dev-summit-north-america-2026-schedule), 2026-04-02)
- **[NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI on RTX Hardware]** — NVIDIA announced RTX AI Garage support for Google's Gemma 4 models, enabling local agentic AI inference on consumer RTX GPUs and furthering the push toward on-device AI agent capabilities without cloud dependencies. (Source: [NVIDIA Blog](https://blogs.nvidia.com/blog/rtx-ai-garage-open-models-google-gemma-4/), 2026-04-02)

### AI Governance
- **[IAPP Global Summit 2026 Concludes as Largest Digital Responsibility Gathering Ever]** — The IAPP Global Summit concluded in Washington DC with over 15,000 attendees, tripling prior years' attendance, covering convergence of privacy, AI governance, and cybersecurity law. Author Salman Rushdie keynoted on the notion of privacy after the attempt on his life. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/conference/iapp-global-summit), 2026-04-02)
- **[Microsoft Publishes Adaptive AI Governance Framework]** — Microsoft released a practical framework for building trustworthy AI through adaptive governance, emphasizing iterative risk assessment, organizational AI readiness, and governance controls that evolve with AI system capabilities. (Source: [Microsoft Power Platform Blog](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/power-platform/blog/2026/04/01/building-trustworthy-ai-a-practical-framework-for-adaptive-governance/), 2026-04-01)
- **[EU AI Act Full Application Countdown: Four Months to August 2, 2026]** — With the EU AI Act's full application date approaching, organizations face increasing pressure to complete risk classifications, document AI inventories, and implement required governance controls for high-risk AI systems, as regulators shift from principle to enforceable rules. (Source: [European Commission](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/regulatory-framework-ai), 2026)

### Data Governance
- **[Federal Data Readiness Challenges Threaten AI Adoption Across Government]** — Despite the White House AI Action Plan's push for expanded AI infrastructure, federal agencies are struggling to operationalize AI due to fragmented data environments, inconsistent standards, and incomplete or inaccurate data limiting the reliability and scalability of AI models. (Source: [Federal News Network](https://federalnewsnetwork.com/commentary/2026/04/the-data-challenge-impacting-federal-ai-adoption/), 2026-04-03)
- **[Privacy Rights Clearinghouse Publishes 2026 50-State Data Breach Notification Survey]** — The updated comprehensive survey documents the evolving patchwork of state breach notification requirements, including California's new 30-day individual notice and 15-day AG reporting requirements and Oklahoma's expanded definitions. (Source: [Privacy Rights Clearinghouse](https://privacyrights.org/resources-tools/reports/data-breach-notification-laws-50-state-survey-2026-edition), 2026)
- **[State Privacy Enforcement Enters Aggressive Phase as Cure Periods Expire]** — With Montana's cure period expired April 1 and upcoming expirations in Minnesota, Nebraska, and New Jersey, state attorneys general now have direct enforcement powers without grace periods, signaling the most aggressive U.S. privacy enforcement climate in history. (Source: [Smith Law](https://www.smithlaw.com/newsroom/publications/data-privacy-in-2026-state-enforcement-takes-center-stage), 2026-04)

## 2026-04-02

### Databricks
- **[Databricks Partners with STATION F to Accelerate AI Adoption for European Startups]** — Databricks announced a partnership with STATION F, the world's largest startup campus, to help European founders build AI applications at scale using Lakebase, Databricks' serverless Postgres database. The partnership provides founders with additional insights, skills, and tools to scale AI apps and agents with governance and security. (Source: [Databricks Newsroom](https://www.databricks.com/company/newsroom/press-releases/databricks-joins-station-f-accelerate-ai-adoption-european-founders), 2026-04-02)
- **[2026 Built-On Databricks Startup Challenge Launched with $1M+ in Prizes]** — Databricks Ventures and Databricks for Startups launched the 2026 Built-On Databricks Startup Challenge, a global competition for early-stage B2B startups building on the Databricks platform, with over $1 million in prizes including up to $500,000 in product credits. Applications are open until May 22 with winners announced at the Data + AI Summit in June. (Source: [Databricks Blog](https://www.databricks.com/blog/announcing-2026-built-databricks-startup-challenge), 2026-04-01)
- **[GPT-4o Fully Retired from Databricks-Hosted Model Serving]** — OpenAI's GPT-4o is being fully retired across all plans as of April 3, 2026, completing the transition to GPT-5.x series models on Mosaic AI Model Serving. ChatGPT Business, Enterprise, and Edu customers retained access within Custom GPTs until this deadline. (Source: [OpenAI Help Center](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001051-retiring-gpt-4o-and-other-chatgpt-models), 2026-04-03)

### AI Innovation
- **[Anthropic Accidentally Leaks Claude Code Source Code — 512,000 Lines Exposed]** — A Claude Code 2.1.88 update accidentally included internal source code, exposing approximately 1,900 files and 512,000 lines of TypeScript. Before takedown, the code was uploaded to GitHub and copied over 50,000 times, revealing unreleased features including a "Proactive mode" and a persistent background agent codenamed "Kairos." (Source: [Bloomberg](https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2026-04-01/anthropic-accidentally-releases-source-code-for-claude-ai-agent), 2026-04-01)
- **[Claude Operon Life Sciences Model Leaked Ahead of Official Announcement]** — Anthropic's Claude Operon, a specialized model for computational biology, was leaked ahead of its official announcement. The model is designed for tasks including constructing phylogenetic trees, optimizing CRISPR sequences, and analyzing RNA sequencing data. (Source: [Geeky Gadgets](https://www.geeky-gadgets.com/claude-operon-leak/), 2026-04-02)
- **[Google Releases Veo 3.1 Lite for Affordable High-Volume Video Generation]** — Google launched Veo 3.1 Lite, a cheaper and faster video generation model designed for developers building high-volume video products. The release complements the broader Gemini 3.1 model family including Flash-Lite and Flash Live for real-time voice assistance. (Source: [Hipther](https://hipther.com/latest-news/2026/04/01/109560/ai-dispatch-daily-trends-and-innovations-april-1-2026-google-veo-3-1-lite-openai-linkedin-and-realbotix/), 2026-04-01)
- **[GPT-5.5 "Spud" Completes Pretraining, Q2 Release Expected]** — OpenAI has finalized pretraining of its next-generation model codenamed "Spud," expected to launch as GPT-5.5 or GPT-6. Safety evaluations and red-teaming are underway, with a Q2 2026 public release most likely based on prior cadence. (Source: [RenovateQR](https://renovateqr.com/blog/ai-models-april-2026), 2026-04-01)

### AI Governance
- **[IAPP Global Summit 2026 Opens with Privacy and AI Governance Awards]** — The IAPP announced its 2026 privacy and digital responsibility award winners at the Global Summit in Washington, D.C., with Vanguard Awards to professionals from IBM, Hogan Lovells, and EY, and the Privacy Leadership Award to CDT President Alexandra Reeve Givens. Over 5,000 attendees are discussing the convergence of privacy, AI governance, and cybersecurity. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/iapp-announces-2026-privacy-and-digital-responsibility-award-winners/), 2026-04-01)
- **[EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework Second Review Set for 2027]** — At the IAPP Global Summit, U.S. and EU officials confirmed that the European Commission's second review of the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework will take place in 2027, examining FTC enforcement implementation and technical solutions for transatlantic data transfers. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/iapp-global-summit-2026-on-the-state-of-the-trans-atlantic-data-transfer-agreement), 2026-04-01)
- **[Washington State Passes Five AI Bills Including Chatbot Safety and Deepfake Protections]** — Washington's legislature gave final approval to five AI-related bills covering AI content disclosure (HB 1170), chatbot safety for minors (HB 2225), AI in health insurance prior authorization (SB 5395), AI deepfakes and minors (SB 5105), and digital likeness protections (SB 5886). (Source: [Kiteworks](https://www.kiteworks.com/regulatory-compliance/state-ai-legislation-2026-compliance-data-governance/), 2026-03-12)
- **[SEC 2026 Examination Priorities Elevate AI Above Cryptocurrency as Top Risk]** — The SEC's 2026 examination priorities now list AI and cybersecurity above cryptocurrency as the dominant risk topic, reflecting AI's shift from an emerging fintech area to a clear area of operational risk linked to disclosures and internal use for critical functions. (Source: [RegTech Analyst](https://regtechanalyst.com/69-of-firms-warn-ai-will-drive-compliance-risks-in-2026/), 2026-04-01)

### Data Governance
- **[Italy Fines Intesa Sanpaolo €31.8M Over Systemic Insider Data Breach]** — Italy's data protection authority fined Intesa Sanpaolo €31.8 million ($36.4M) after a single employee made over 6,600 unauthorized queries accessing 3,573 customers' banking data over two years without detection, highlighting critical failures in insider threat monitoring and privileged access governance. (Source: [The Record](https://therecord.media/italian-regulator-fines-financial-giant-36-million), 2026-03-30)
- **[Montana Consumer Data Privacy Act Cure Period Expires April 1, 2026]** — Montana's MTCDPA right-to-cure period expired on April 1, meaning the Montana Attorney General can now bring enforcement actions for violations without first giving businesses an opportunity to fix the issue. (Source: [VERASafe](https://verasafe.com/blog/how-organizations-can-prepare-for-u-s-privacy-laws-in-2026/), 2026-04-01)
- **[GDPR Procedural Regulation Enters Into Force, Harmonizes Cross-Border Enforcement]** — The new GDPR Procedural Regulation took effect January 1, 2026, harmonizing complaint admissibility conditions, introducing a simple cooperation procedure, and setting a 15-month resolution timeframe for cross-border cases, with full changes applying from April 2, 2027. (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/digital-governance-in-2026-entropy-and-regulatory-complexity), 2026-04-01)
- **[Multistate Privacy Enforcement Consortium Formed by U.S. State AGs]** — U.S. state attorneys general entered a memorandum of understanding to share expertise and coordinate investigations into privacy violations collectively, signaling an era of more aggressive multistate enforcement similar to the data breach enforcement framework of the 2010s. (Source: [ArentFox Schiff](https://www.afslaw.com/perspectives/privacy-counsel/new-era-us-privacy-enforcement-has-only-just-begun-2025-trends-and), 2026-04-01)

## 2026-04-01

### Databricks
- **[Databricks Runtime 18.1 Reaches General Availability]** — Databricks Runtime 18.1 is now generally available, delivering performance improvements and expanded compatibility across workloads. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-31)
- **[Account-Level Databricks One Launches in Beta]** — Databricks One is now available in Beta, providing a single entry point to discover and interact with AI/BI dashboards, Genie spaces, and Databricks Apps across all workspaces in an account, with unified Chat powered by Genie routing. (Source: [Databricks AI/BI Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/ai-bi/release-notes/2026), 2026-03-31)
- **[Lakeflow Connect Workday HCM Connector Enters Beta]** — The managed Workday HCM connector in Lakeflow Connect is now available in Beta across AWS, Azure, and GCP, enabling direct ingestion of Workday Human Capital Management data into Databricks with incremental reads. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-31)
- **[Multi-Table Atomic Transactions Now Supported in Unity Catalog]** — Unity Catalog now supports grouping multiple SQL statements across multiple tables into a single atomic transaction using BEGIN ATOMIC...END syntax, ensuring all changes succeed or roll back together. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-31)
- **[Table Health Indicators Launch in Catalog Explorer Public Preview]** — Health indicators for tables are now visible on schema and table overview pages in Catalog Explorer, showing freshness and completeness status based on anomaly detection. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-31)

### AI Innovation
- **[Microsoft Introduces Multi-Model Intelligence in Copilot Researcher]** — Microsoft launched "Critique" and "Council" capabilities for Copilot Researcher: Critique uses GPT for drafting and Claude for review to reduce hallucinations, while Council provides side-by-side model comparisons, yielding a 13.8% improvement in deep research accuracy. (Source: [Microsoft Tech Community](https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/microsoft365copilotblog/introducing-multi-model-intelligence-in-researcher/4506011), 2026-03-30)
- **[OutSystems Introduces Agentic Systems Engineering for Enterprise AI]** — OutSystems announced an "Enterprise Context Graph" that lets AI coding agents (Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Cursor) operate within shared guardrails, with early customer SRS Distribution reporting 50% faster app development. Early access opens Q2 2026. (Source: [BusinessWire](https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260331236008/en/), 2026-03-31)
- **[Runway Launches $10M Venture Fund and Builders Program for AI Startups]** — Runway, valued at $5.3B, launched a $10 million fund for pre-seed and seed-stage AI startups alongside a Builders program offering 500K free API credits and access to its real-time video agent API "Characters." (Source: [TechCrunch](https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/31/exclusive-runway-launches-10m-fund-builders-program-to-support-early-stage-ai-startups/), 2026-03-31)
- **[Bessemer Publishes AI Infrastructure Roadmap: Five Frontiers for 2026]** — Bessemer Venture Partners identified five critical AI infrastructure frontiers: AI harnesses, continual learning, inference optimization, reinforcement learning platforms, and world models, noting inference now accounts for 60-70% of total AI compute demand. (Source: [Bessemer Venture Partners](https://www.bvp.com/atlas/ai-infrastructure-roadmap-five-frontiers-for-2026), 2026-03-31)
- **[Microsoft Agent 365 Offering Launches April 1]** — Microsoft's Agent 365 becomes available across Immersion Briefings, envisioning and proof-of-concept engagements, and Deployment Accelerators to accelerate enterprise AI agent adoption within Microsoft 365. (Source: [Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/partner-center/announcements/2026-march), 2026-04-01)

### AI Governance
- **[FTC Commissioner Meador Stresses Case-by-Case AI Enforcement at IAPP Global Summit]** — At the IAPP Global Summit 2026, FTC Commissioner Mark Meador stated the agency will not issue prescriptive AI regulations, preferring case-by-case enforcement focused on AI-powered scams and impersonation, noting AI is "lowering the barriers to entry into scamming." (Source: [IAPP](https://iapp.org/news/a/iapp-global-summit-2026-ftc-commissioner-meador-stresses-agency-preference-for-case-by-case-enforcement), 2026-03-31)
- **[UK Financial Reporting Council Publishes First Global AI Audit Guidance]** — The FRC released the first audit regulator guidance globally on using generative and agentic AI in audits, emphasizing human auditors remain fully accountable for audit quality and identifying risks including misinterpretation of AI outputs and methodologies falling short of standards. (Source: [FRC](https://www.frc.org.uk/news-and-events/news/2026/03/innovative-new-guidance-supports-audit-firm-adoption-of-emerging-ai-technologies/), 2026-03-30)
- **[McKinsey "State of AI Trust in 2026" Finds 80% of Organizations Encountering Risky Agent Behavior]** — McKinsey's annual survey of ~500 organizations found Responsible AI maturity rose to 2.3 (from 2.0 in 2025), but 80% have encountered risky behavior from AI agents. The report adds "agentic AI governance" as a new fifth dimension of its trust maturity model. (Source: [McKinsey](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/tech-and-ai/our-insights/tech-forward/state-of-ai-trust-in-2026-shifting-to-the-agentic-era), 2026-03)
- **[69% of Firms Warn AI Will Drive Compliance Risks in 2026]** — A global eflow study found nearly seven in ten firms believe accelerating AI use will lead to compliance issues within 12 months, with the SEC's 2026 examination priorities now listing AI and cybersecurity above cryptocurrency as the dominant risk topic. (Source: [RegTech Analyst](https://regtechanalyst.com/69-of-firms-warn-ai-will-drive-compliance-risks-in-2026/), 2026-03)

### Data Governance
- **[World Data Organization (WDO) Officially Established in Beijing]** — The world's first professional international organization dedicated to data development and governance held its inaugural general meeting on March 30 with over 200 founding members, aiming to harmonize cross-border data policies and reduce multinational compliance costs. (Source: [Xinhua](https://english.news.cn/20260330/6f2386b6460b4408ae4ea9b6a5fa92b2/c.html), 2026-03-30)
- **[FTC Takes Action Against Match Group and OkCupid for Sharing User Data Without Consent]** — The FTC filed a complaint alleging OkCupid shared nearly three million user photos and location data with a financially connected third party, violating its own privacy policy. The settlement permanently prohibits misrepresenting data practices. (Source: [FTC](https://www.ftc.gov/news-events/news/press-releases/2026/03/ftc-takes-action-against-match-okcupid-deceiving-users-sharing-personal-data-third-party), 2026-03-30)
- **[Maryland Online Data Privacy Act Enforcement Begins April 1, 2026]** — One of the strictest U.S. state privacy frameworks becomes enforceable today, prohibiting sale of sensitive personal information, barring data sale and targeted advertising to minors under 18, and imposing strict data minimization requirements with a 60-day cure period through April 1, 2027. (Source: [Verified Credentials](https://news.verifiedcredentials.com/maryland-online-data-privacy-act-enforcement-begins-on-april-1-2026), 2026-04-01)
- **[Bank Director 2026 Risk Survey: AI Exposes Data Governance Knowledge Gaps]** — The survey of bank CEOs, board members, and CROs found 79% cite fraud as a top risk with 84% concerned about AI-enabled scams, while a third say they do not understand agentic AI at all, highlighting oversight challenges. (Source: [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/bank-directors-2026-risk-survey-ai-exposes-threats-knowledge-gaps-302730067.html), 2026-03-31)

## 2026-03-31

### Databricks
- **[Databricks Announces $850M UK Investment to Accelerate Enterprise AI Adoption]** — Databricks announced plans to invest more than $850 million in the UK over three years, quadrupling its London office footprint with a new 137,000 sq ft Fitzrovia headquarters and committing to train 100,000 people in data and AI skills by 2028. (Source: [Databricks Newsroom](https://www.databricks.com/company/newsroom/press-releases/databricks-announces-850m-uk-investment-accelerate-enterprise-data), 2026-03-31)
- **[Databricks Launches Lakewatch Agentic SIEM with Antimatter and SiftD.ai Acquisitions]** — Databricks entered the security market with the launch of Lakewatch, an open agentic SIEM now in Private Preview, alongside acquisitions of Antimatter and SiftD.ai to bolster its security analytics capabilities. (Source: [Databricks Newsroom](https://www.databricks.com/company/newsroom/press-releases/databricks-enters-security-market-launch-lakewatch-new-open-agentic), 2026-03-24)
- **[Governed Tags and Sample Data Explorer with Genie Code Reach GA]** — Governed Tags became generally available in mid-March, allowing administrators to define controlled tag sets for Unity Catalog objects, while Sample Data Explorer with Genie Code reached GA in late March, enabling natural language queries on Unity Catalog tables. (Source: [Databricks Release Notes](https://docs.databricks.com/aws/en/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-31)
- **[Mosaic AI Model Serving Adds OpenAI GPT-5.4 Mini and Nano]** — Mosaic AI Model Serving now supports OpenAI GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano as Databricks-hosted models, accessible via Foundation Model APIs with pay-per-token pricing. (Source: [Azure Databricks Release Notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/release-notes/product/2026/march), 2026-03-31)

### AI Innovation
- **[Anthropic Approaches $19B Annualized Revenue, Preps Public Listing]** — Anthropic is approaching $19 billion in annualized revenue and is reportedly taking early steps toward a public offering, potentially as soon as late 2026, as the AI lab continues to scale rapidly. (Source: [FutureTech AI Marketing](https://blog.tahababa.com/2026/03/march-31-2026-ai-news-roundup-google.html), 2026-03-31)
- **[AWS Launches Agent Plugin for Serverless]** — AWS announced the Agent Plugin for AWS Serverless, allowing developers to build, deploy, and manage serverless applications using AI coding assistants like Kiro, Claude Code, and Cursor by packaging skills, sub-agents, and MCP servers into modular units. (Source: [AWS News Blog](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/aws/aws-weekly-roundup-aws-ai-ml-scholars-program-agent-plugin-for-aws-serverless-and-more-march-30-2026/), 2026-03-30)
- **[Atlassian Lays Off 10% of Workforce for AI Pivot]** — Atlassian announced layoffs of roughly 1,600 employees (10% of workforce) to redirect resources toward AI development, replacing its CTO with two new AI-focused CTOs. (Source: [The Neuron](https://www.theneuron.ai/ai-news-digests/-around-the-horn-digest-everything-that-happened-in-ai-this-weekend-march-28-29-2026/), 2026-03-29)
- **[MCP Crosses 97 Million Installs]** — The Model Context Protocol crossed 97 million installs in March 2026, signaling its transition from experimental standard to foundational agentic infrastructure, with every major AI provider now shipping MCP-compatible tooling. (Source: [Digital Applied](https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/march-2026-ai-roundup-month-that-changed-everything), 2026-03-30)
- **[Y Combinator Winter 2026: 85% AI-First Startups]** — The Winter 2026 Y Combinator demo day showcased AI's dominance with 85% of presenting companies being AI-first, the highest proportion in YC's history. (Source: [LLM Stats](https://llm-stats.com/ai-news), 2026-03-31)

### AI Governance
- **[California Governor Newsom Signs AI Executive Order on State Contracts]** — Governor Newsom signed an executive order requiring companies seeking California state contracts to implement safeguards against AI misuse, including protections against illegal content creation, harmful bias, and civil rights violations, with a certification framework due within 120 days. (Source: [Benzinga](https://www.benzinga.com/markets/tech/26/03/51559472/california-governor-gavin-newsom-cracks-down-on-ai-misuse-with-new-order-mandating-safeguards-while-trump-pressure-companies), 2026-03-31)
- **[IAPP Global Privacy Summit 2026 Opens in Washington DC]** — The world's largest privacy conference opened with over 5,000 attendees, 300+ speakers, and 70+ sessions focused on AI governance, US state privacy law patchwork, and enforcement actions, with Prince Harry keynoting on digital society. (Source: [State of Surveillance](https://stateofsurveillance.org/news/iapp-global-privacy-summit-2026-ai-governance-preview/), 2026-03-30)
- **[Collibra/Harris Poll: 90% Support Federal AI Disclosure Requirements]** — A Harris Poll survey of 300+ US tech decision makers found 90% support federal requirements for companies to disclose and document high-risk AI systems, 93% believe companies should be required to disclose AI tool usage, and 84% say organizations must increase AI spending to remain competitive. (Source: [PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/new-collibra-survey-by-the-harris-poll-finds-84-of-decision-makers-say-organizations-must-increase-ai-spending-in-2026-to-remain-competitive-with-big-tech-with-almost-half-saying-ai-is-falling-short-302727628.html), 2026-03-30)
- **[India Hosts Global AI Governance Summit in New Delhi]** — India is hosting a high-level global summit bringing together world leaders and tech executives to discuss creating a unified international framework for AI safety and ensuring equitable distribution of AI benefits among developing nations. (Source: [FutureTech AI Marketing](https://blog.tahababa.com/2026/03/march-31-2026-ai-news-roundup-google.html), 2026-03-31)

### Data Governance
- **[European Commission Confirms Data Breach from March 24 Cyberattack]** — The European Commission confirmed that the March 24 attack on infrastructure supporting the Europa web platform resulted in a data breach, after initial statements said the incident had been contained. (Source: [Digital Forensics Magazine](https://digitalforensicsmagazine.com/news-roundup-30th-march-2026/), 2026-03-30)
- **[FBI Director Patel's Personal Email Breached by Iranian-Linked Actors]** — The FBI confirmed that Iranian-linked threat actors breached FBI Director Kash Patel's personal email account, with published materials reportedly dating from earlier years. (Source: [Digital Forensics Magazine](https://digitalforensicsmagazine.com/news-roundup-30th-march-2026/), 2026-03-29)
- **[Lloyds Banking Group IT Glitch Exposes 448,000 Customer Records]** — Nearly 448,000 Lloyds Banking Group customers were affected by an IT glitch that exposed transaction data and some personal details to other users, highlighting systemic risks in financial services data management. (Source: [Reuters](https://www.reuters.com), 2026-03-27)
- **[California SB 446 Introduces Accelerated Breach Notification Requirements]** — California's 2026 amendment to its breach notification law introduces 30-day individual notice and 15-day attorney general reporting requirements, signaling a broader trend toward faster, more transparent consumer notification. (Source: [SharkStriker](https://sharkstriker.com/blog/march-data-breaches-today-2026/), 2026-03-30)
